title: dudleya abramsii subsp. murina
intro: Dudleya abramsii subsp. murina, also known as the Abrams' dudleya, is a rare succulent plant species endemic to the southwestern United States. It is a type of succulent that belongs to the family Crassulaceae and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, burns, and skin conditions, due to its ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Dudleya abramsii subsp. murina can be made into salves, ointments, or infusions, and are often used topically to promote wound healing and skin health.
